### Create Remix Project

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/remix.mdx

Initializes a new Remix application using the official create-remix CLI tool.

```bash
npx create-remix@latest my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Create a universal registry item with multiple files

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

This example shows how to define a universal registry item that installs multiple files. Each file within the "files" array requires an explicit "target" path for framework-agnostic installation.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "my-custom-starter-template",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "dependencies": ["better-auth"],
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file-01.json",
      "type": "registry:file",
      "target": "~/file-01.json",
      "content": "..."
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file-02.vue",
      "type": "registry:file",
      "target": "~/pages/file-02.vue",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Get Component Documentation with shadcn CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/SKILL.md

Fetch documentation and example URLs for a specific component. Replace `<component>` with the actual component name.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs <component>
```

--------------------------------

### Get shadcn/ui component documentation from CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2026-03-cli-v4.mdx

Retrieves direct links to documentation, examples, and API references for any specified UI component.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs combobox

combobox
  - docs      https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/combobox
  - examples  https://ui.shadcn.com/code/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/combobox-example.tsx
  - api       https://base-ui.com/react/components/combobox
```

--------------------------------

### Use Registry Target Placeholders for Component Installation

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/registry-item-json.mdx

Use placeholders like @ui/, @components/, @lib/, and @hooks/ to install files into user-configured directories without hardcoding paths. Placeholders must be at the start of the target path.

```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/button.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@ui/button.tsx"
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/prompt-input.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@ui/ai/prompt-input.tsx"
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/card.tsx",
      "type": "registry:component",
      "target": "@components/card.tsx"
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/helper.ts",
      "type": "registry:lib",
      "target": "@lib/helper.ts"
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/use-demo.ts",
      "type": "registry:hook",
      "target": "@hooks/use-demo.ts"
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### CLI init

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/cli.md

Initializes shadcn/ui in an existing project or creates a new project when the name flag is provided. It can optionally install components during the setup process.

```APIDOC
## CLI init

### Description
Initializes shadcn/ui in an existing project or creates a new project (when --name is provided). Optionally installs components in the same step.

### Method
CLI

### Endpoint
npx shadcn@latest init [components...]

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Optional - Template (next, start, vite, next-monorepo, react-router)
- **preset** (string) - Optional - Preset configuration (named, code, or URL)
- **yes** (boolean) - Optional - Skip confirmation prompt (Default: true)
- **defaults** (boolean) - Optional - Use defaults (Default: false)
- **force** (boolean) - Optional - Force overwrite existing configuration (Default: false)
- **cwd** (string) - Optional - Working directory (Default: current)
- **name** (string) - Optional - Name for new project
- **silent** (boolean) - Optional - Mute output (Default: false)
- **rtl** (boolean) - Optional - Enable RTL support
- **reinstall** (boolean) - Optional - Re-install existing UI components (Default: false)
- **monorepo** (boolean) - Optional - Scaffold a monorepo project

### Request Example
npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --template next --defaults
```

--------------------------------

### Install Radix UI dependencies manually

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/toggle-group.mdx

Install the core Radix UI library as a dependency for manual setup.

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Get Current Project Context with shadcn CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/SKILL.md

Retrieve the project configuration and list of installed components in JSON format. Use this to understand the current project setup before adding or modifying components.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest info --json
```

--------------------------------

### Install Hover Card via CLI or Manual Steps

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/hover-card.mdx

Use the CLI for automatic installation or manually install the Radix UI dependency.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add hover-card
```

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Examples Directory Structure

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/examples/README.md

Illustrates the typical directory layout for example components, categorizing them into 'base' and 'radix' and showing the auto-generated index file.

```text
examples
├── base
│   ├── button-demo.tsx
│   └── ...
├── radix
│   └── ...
└── __index__.tsx    # Auto-generated by registry:build
```

--------------------------------

### Install Combobox via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/combobox.mdx

Quick installation using the shadcn CLI tool.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add combobox
```

--------------------------------

### Install Resizable component using shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/resizable.mdx

Use the shadcn/ui CLI to quickly add the Resizable component to your project, automating setup.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add resizable
```

--------------------------------

### Install Alert Dialog

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/alert-dialog.mdx

Install the component using the shadcn CLI or manually via npm.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add alert-dialog
```

```bash
npm install @base-ui/react
```

--------------------------------

### Registry Item with Target Placeholders

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

Use `files[].target` placeholders like `@ui/` and `@lib/` to specify installation paths relative to the user's configured shadcn directories. This example shows a child registry item installing a UI component and a helper.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "alias-child",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/alias/target-alias-button.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@ui/target-alias-button.tsx",
      "content": "..."
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/alias/target-alias-helper.ts",
      "type": "registry:lib",
      "target": "@lib/target-alias-helper.ts",
      "content": "..."
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/alias/prompt-input.tsx",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@ui/ai/prompt-input.tsx",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Get component documentation URLs with docs command

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/cli.md

Retrieve resolved URLs for component documentation, examples, and API references. Accepts one or more component names as arguments.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs <components...> [options]
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs input button
```

--------------------------------

### Run Registry Build Command

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/examples/README.md

Command to regenerate the example index after adding or removing example files, ensuring new components are discoverable.

```bash
pnpm --filter=v4 registry:build
```

--------------------------------

### Install Menubar

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/menubar.mdx

Install the component using the CLI or manually by adding the Radix UI dependency.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add menubar
```

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Get shadcn/ui Component Documentation URLs

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/SKILL.md

Use this command to retrieve documentation, examples, and API reference URLs for specified shadcn/ui components. Always run this before working with components to ensure correct API usage.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs button dialog select
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dropdown Menu

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/dropdown-menu.mdx

Install the component using the shadcn CLI or manually add the Radix UI primitive dependency.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add dropdown-menu
```

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Example of multiple registry configuration in components.json

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/components-json.mdx

Illustrate a `components.json` setup with multiple registries, combining public, private, and team-specific sources with authentication and parameters.

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@shadcn": "https://ui.shadcn.com/r/{name}.json",
    "@company-ui": {
      "url": "https://registry.company.com/ui/{name}.json",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer ${COMPANY_TOKEN}"
      }
    },
    "@team": {
      "url": "https://team.company.com/{name}.json",
      "params": {
        "team": "frontend",
        "version": "${REGISTRY_VERSION}"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dialog component

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/dialog.mdx

Choose between the automated CLI installation or manual dependency management for Radix UI primitives.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add dialog
```

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Create a universal registry item for ESLint configuration

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

This example demonstrates a universal registry item that installs a custom ESLint configuration file. The "target" field ensures the file is placed in a specific, framework-agnostic location.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "my-eslint-config",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/your/registry/default/custom-eslint.json",
      "type": "registry:file",
      "target": "~/.eslintrc.json",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install core dependencies

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/manual.mdx

Install the necessary packages for shadcn/ui components, including styling utilities and icons.

```bash
npm install shadcn class-variance-authority clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react tw-animate-css
```

--------------------------------

### Install Sidebar via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/sidebar.mdx

Command-line installation of the sidebar component using shadcn.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add sidebar
```

--------------------------------

### Installation

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/input-group.mdx

Instructions for installing the Input Group component in your project using either the CLI command or manual installation method.

```APIDOC
## Installation

### CLI Installation
```bash
npx shadcn@latest add input-group
```

### Manual Installation
1. Copy and paste the following code into your project
2. Component file: `components/ui/input-group.tsx`
3. Update the import paths to match your project setup

### Import
```tsx
import {
  InputGroup,
  InputGroupAddon,
  InputGroupButton,
  InputGroupInput,
  InputGroupText,
  InputGroupTextarea,
} from "@/components/ui/input-group"
```
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start Project with shadcn/create Preset

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/tanstack.mdx

Use the generated command from shadcn/create to initialize a new TanStack Start project with a pre-configured preset.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --preset [CODE] --template start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Input OTP

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/input-otp.mdx

Add the component to your project using the shadcn CLI or by manually installing the underlying dependency.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add input-otp
```

```bash
npm install input-otp
```

--------------------------------

### Install Navigation Menu via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/navigation-menu.mdx

Quick installation command for adding the navigation-menu component to a shadcn/ui project.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add navigation-menu
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn-ui CLI with init command

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2023-06-new-cli.mdx

Run the init command to configure components.json with project settings including style, base color, CSS file location, theming method, Tailwind config path, import aliases, and React Server Components preference. This interactive setup creates a configuration file that controls component installation and styling behavior.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init
```

--------------------------------

### Install Alert Dialog

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/alert-dialog.mdx

Add the component to your project using the shadcn CLI or by manually installing the Radix UI dependency.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add alert-dialog
```

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/empty.mdx

Install the Empty component using the shadcn CLI command.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add empty
```

### Install Radix UI dependency manually

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/button.mdx

Manual installation step for the Radix UI dependency when not using the CLI.

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a shadcn project

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/cli.mdx

Use the `init` command to set up configuration and dependencies for a new or existing project. This command installs necessary packages, adds the `cn` utility, and configures CSS variables.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest create
```

--------------------------------

### Install Kbd component via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/kbd.mdx

Quick installation command for adding the Kbd component to your shadcn/ui project.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add kbd
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn/ui project

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/components-json.mdx

Run this command to create a `components.json` file in your project, which is required for using the CLI to add components.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init
```

--------------------------------

### Add and Implement Button Component

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/remix.mdx

Commands to add the Button component to the project via CLI and an example of how to import and render it within a Remix route file.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add button
```

```tsx
import { Button } from "~/components/ui/button"

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <div>
      <Button>Click me</Button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manually install react-resizable-panels dependency

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/resizable.mdx

Install the core `react-resizable-panels` library as a direct dependency for manual setup.

```bash
npm install react-resizable-panels
```

--------------------------------

### Install Recharts Dependency Manually

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/chart.mdx

Install the core Recharts library as a dependency for manual chart setup.

```bash
npm install recharts
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start project with RTL support using shadcn/create CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/rtl/start.mdx

This command initializes a new TanStack Start project with Right-to-Left (RTL) support enabled. It uses the `shadcn/create` CLI tool with the `start` template and the `--rtl` flag. This step can be skipped if the project was already created using `shadcn/create`.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest create --template start --rtl
```

--------------------------------

### Install Carousel component

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/carousel.mdx

Use the CLI for automatic setup or install the Embla Carousel dependency manually.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add carousel
```

```bash
npm install embla-carousel-react
```

--------------------------------

### Registry File Structure Example

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2026-05-registry-include.mdx

Illustrates the recommended file structure for organizing a large source registry across multiple `registry.json` files, enabling modular composition.

```txt
registry.json
components
└── ui
    ├── button.tsx
    ├── input.tsx
    └── registry.json
hooks
├── registry.json
├── use-media-query.ts
└── use-toggle.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Install Accordion via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/accordion.mdx

Quick installation command for the Accordion component using shadcn CLI.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add accordion
```

--------------------------------

### Install Checkbox component using shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/checkbox.mdx

Installs the Checkbox component and its dependencies using the shadcn/ui command-line interface, simplifying the setup process.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add checkbox
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Next.js via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/next.mdx

Scaffold a new project using the init command. Include the --monorepo flag for monorepo configurations.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t next
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t next --monorepo
```

--------------------------------

### Import and Use shadcn/ui Card Component in TanStack Start

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/tanstack.mdx

Example of importing and rendering the `Card` component within a TanStack Start route file, demonstrating basic usage.

```tsx
import { createFileRoute } from "@tanstack/react-router"

import {
  Card,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
}
from "@/components/ui/card"

export const Route = createFileRoute("/")({
  component: App,
})

function App() {
  return (
    <Card className="max-w-sm">
      <CardHeader>
        <CardTitle>Project Overview</CardTitle>
        <CardDescription>
          Track progress and recent activity for your TanStack Start app.
        </CardDescription>
      </CardHeader>
      <CardContent>
        Your design system is ready. Start building your next component.
      </CardContent>
    </Card>
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn/ui for new Vite project using CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/vite.mdx

Scaffold a new Vite project and configure shadcn/ui by running the `init` command. Use the `--monorepo` flag for monorepo setups.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t vite
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t vite --monorepo
```

--------------------------------

### Install Hover Card via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/hover-card.mdx

Quick installation command for adding the Hover Card component to a shadcn/ui project.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add hover-card
```

--------------------------------

### Install Radix UI Dependencies (Manual)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/radix/accordion.mdx

Manually install the core Radix UI primitive required for the Accordion component.

```bash
npm install radix-ui
```

--------------------------------

### shadcn init command options

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/cli.mdx

Displays the available options for the `shadcn init` command, including arguments for components, templates, presets, and various configuration flags.

```bash
Usage: shadcn init [options] [components...]

initialize your project and install dependencies

Arguments:
  components                 names, url or local path to component

Options:
  -t, --template <template>  the template to use. (next, vite, start, react-router, laravel, astro)
  -b, --base <base>          the component library to use. (radix, base)
  -p, --preset [name]        use a preset configuration
  -y, --yes                  skip confirmation prompt. (default: true)
  -d, --defaults             use default configuration: --template=next --preset=nova (default: false)
  -f, --force                force overwrite of existing configuration. (default: false)
  -c, --cwd <cwd>            the working directory. defaults to the current directory.
  -n, --name <name>          the name for the new project.
  -s, --silent               mute output. (default: false)
  --css-variables            use css variables for theming. (default: true)
  --no-css-variables         do not use css variables for theming.
  --monorepo                 scaffold a monorepo project.
  --no-monorepo              skip the monorepo prompt.
  --rtl                      enable RTL support.
  --no-rtl                   disable RTL support.
  --pointer                  enable pointer cursor for buttons.
  --no-pointer               disable pointer cursor for buttons.
  --reinstall                re-install existing UI components.
  --no-reinstall             do not re-install existing UI components.
  -h, --help                 display help for command
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Server with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/_blocks.mdx

Start the development server to preview blocks in real-time during development. The server will watch for file changes and hot-reload the application.

```bash
pnpm www:dev
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize or Create a Project with shadcn CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/cli.md

Use this command to initialize shadcn/ui in an existing project or create a new one, optionally installing components in the same step.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init [components...] [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Project Dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/_blocks.mdx

Install all project dependencies using pnpm package manager. This ensures all required packages and tools are available for development.

```bash
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Install Checkbox Component via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/checkbox.mdx

Quick installation command for adding the Checkbox component to a shadcn/ui project using the command-line interface. This is the recommended method for automatic setup.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add checkbox
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn/ui Project

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/react-router.mdx

Use this command to set up shadcn/ui in your project, configuring necessary files and dependencies.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init
```

--------------------------------

### Install Direction component using shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/direction.mdx

This command uses the shadcn/ui CLI to automatically add the `Direction` component to your project. It handles fetching the component files and integrating them into your setup, simplifying the installation process.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add direction
```

--------------------------------

### Registry Item with Type and Target Mismatch

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

The `target` field determines the file's installation location, even if it differs from the `type` field. This example shows a file with `type: "registry:ui"` being installed into the `@lib/` directory.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "type-mismatch",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/format-date.ts",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@lib/format-date.ts",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new project with shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/index.mdx

Use this command to scaffold a new project directly from the terminal, specifying the target framework.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t [framework]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Toggle component using shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/toggle.mdx

This command installs the Toggle component into your project using the shadcn/ui command-line interface. It automates the process of adding the component files and configuring necessary dependencies, streamlining the setup process.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add toggle
```

--------------------------------

### Display project information with info command

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/cli.md

Show project configuration, framework details, Tailwind setup, and components.json settings. Use this first to discover project structure and resolved paths.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest info [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a shadcn/ui project with template selection

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2026-03-cli-v4.mdx

Interactively select from various full project templates (e.g., Next.js, Vite) when starting a new project.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init

Select a template › - Use arrow-keys. Return to submit.
❯ Next.js
  Vite
  TanStack Start
  React Router
  Astro
  Laravel
```

--------------------------------

### Add All shadcn/ui Components using CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/new.mdx

This command provides a convenient way to add all available shadcn/ui components to your project at once. Using the '--all' flag with the 'add' command ensures that your project includes the entire library of components. This is useful for quickly scaffolding a new project or exploring all available options.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add --all
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize TanStack Start Project with shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/tanstack.mdx

Scaffold a new TanStack Start project using the shadcn/ui CLI, providing options for standard or monorepo configurations.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t start
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t start --monorepo
```

--------------------------------

### Use shadcn/ui Card Component in Astro Page (shadcn/create)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/astro.mdx

Example of importing and using shadcn/ui Card components within an Astro page after setup with shadcn/create.

```astro
---
import Layout from "@/layouts/main.astro"
import {
  Card,
  CardContent,
  CardDescription,
  CardHeader,
  CardTitle,
} from "@/components/ui/card"
---

<Layout>
  <Card className="max-w-sm">
    <CardHeader>
      <CardTitle>Project Overview</CardTitle>
      <CardDescription>
        Track progress and recent activity for your Astro app.
      </CardDescription>
    </CardHeader>
    <CardContent>
      Your design system is ready. Start building your next component.
    </CardContent>
  </Card>
</Layout>
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn dependencies

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/packages/shadcn/README.md

Sets up dependencies, adds the `cn` utility, configures Tailwind CSS, and CSS variables for an existing project.

```bash
npx shadcn init
```

--------------------------------

### Install Aspect Ratio component using shadcn/ui CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/aspect-ratio.mdx

This command adds the Aspect Ratio component to your shadcn/ui project using the command-line interface, automating the setup process.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add aspect-ratio
```

--------------------------------

### Navigate to Project Directory

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/laravel.mdx

Change into your newly created Laravel project directory to proceed with the setup.

```bash
cd my-app
```

--------------------------------

### docs — Get component documentation URLs

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/cli.md

Retrieves resolved URLs for component documentation, examples, and API references. Accepts one or more component names and outputs links to fetch the actual content.

```APIDOC
## CLI Command: docs

### Description
Outputs resolved URLs for component documentation, examples, and API references. Accepts one or more component names.

### Command
```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs <components...> [options]
```

### Parameters
#### Arguments
- **components** (string, variadic) - Required - One or more component names to fetch documentation for

### Usage Example
```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs input button
```

### Response Example
```
base  radix

input
  docs      https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/input
  examples  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../examples/input-example.tsx

button
  docs      https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/button
  examples  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../examples/button-example.tsx
```

### Notes
- Some components include an `api` link to the underlying library (e.g., `cmdk` for the command component)
- The output includes the base library name (e.g., `radix`)
- Each component lists available resources: docs, examples, and optionally api
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize React Router project with CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/react-router.mdx

Scaffolds a new React Router project with shadcn/ui configuration. Use --monorepo flag for monorepo setup.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t react-router
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init -t react-router --monorepo
```

--------------------------------

### Override File Type with Different Target Directory

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/registry-item-json.mdx

The target property can point to a different shadcn directory than the file type, allowing flexible file placement. For example, a registry:ui file can be installed to a @lib/ directory.

```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/example/format-date.ts",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "target": "@lib/format-date.ts"
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Registry Item with Dependencies and Target Placeholders

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

This example demonstrates a parent registry item that depends on another item and uses target placeholders for its own files. It installs an app component and a hook, while its child dependency handles other files.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "alias-parent",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "registryDependencies": ["https://example.com/r/alias-child.json"],
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/alias/target-alias-panel.tsx",
      "type": "registry:component",
      "target": "@components/target-alias-panel.tsx",
      "content": "..."
    },
    {
      "path": "registry/new-york/alias/use-target-alias.ts",
      "type": "registry:hook",
      "target": "@hooks/use-target-alias.ts",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install a resource after quick registry configuration

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/namespace.mdx

Demonstrates installing a resource from a newly configured registry using `npx shadcn` after setting up basic registry mappings in `components.json`. This shows the immediate usability of newly defined namespaces.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add @acme/button
```

--------------------------------

### Quickly configure basic registries in components.json

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/namespace.mdx

A simplified example of adding basic registry configurations to `components.json`, mapping namespaces like `@v0` and `@acme` to their respective URLs. This allows for rapid setup of external resource sources.

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@v0": "https://v0.dev/chat/b/{name}",
    "@acme": "https://registry.acme.com/resources/{name}.json"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Custom Registry Authorization Error Message

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2025-08-cli-3-mcp.mdx

Example of a custom error response provided by registry authors to guide users through authorization issues or expired API keys.

```text
Error:
You are not authorized to access the item at http://example.com/r/component.

Message:
[Unauthorized] Your API key has expired. Renew it at https://example.com/api/renew-key.
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a new shadcn/ui monorepo

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/monorepo.mdx

Run this command to initialize a new shadcn/ui monorepo project with the `--monorepo` flag.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --monorepo
```

--------------------------------

### Create a universal registry item for Python Cursor rules

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/examples.mdx

Define a universal registry item by specifying an explicit "target" path for each file. This example installs custom Cursor rules for Python, making the item framework-agnostic.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://ui.shadcn.com/schema/registry-item.json",
  "name": "python-rules",
  "type": "registry:item",
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/your/registry/default/custom-python.mdc",
      "type": "registry:file",
      "target": "~/.cursor/rules/custom-python.mdc",
      "content": "..."
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Component Documentation via CLI

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2026-04-component-composition.mdx

Use this command to retrieve the full documentation, including composition and examples, for a specific shadcn/ui component like 'card' from the command line.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest docs card
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start project

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/tanstack.mdx

Use the TanStack CLI to create a new project with recommended defaults for Tailwind CSS and import aliases.

```bash
npx @tanstack/cli@latest create
```

--------------------------------

### Install Slider Component using shadcn/ui CLI (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/slider.mdx

This command utilizes the shadcn/ui CLI to automatically add the Slider component and its required dependencies to your project. It streamlines the setup process by handling file generation and package management.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest add slider
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Gatsby project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/gatsby.mdx

This interactive prompt guides the user through setting up a new Gatsby project, specifying TypeScript for language and Tailwind CSS for styling. It covers naming the site and folder, and installing additional features.

```txt
✔ What would you like to call your site?
· your-app-name
✔ What would you like to name the folder where your site will be created?
· your-app-name
✔ Will you be using JavaScript or TypeScript?
· TypeScript
✔ Will you be using a CMS?
· Choose whatever you want
✔ Would you like to install a styling system?
· Tailwind CSS
✔ Would you like to install additional features with other plugins?
· Choose whatever you want
✔ Shall we do this? (Y/n) · Yes
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize a project with a shadcn/ui preset

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/changelog/2026-03-cli-v4.mdx

Scaffolds a new project using a predefined design system configuration, including colors, themes, and fonts, via the --preset flag.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --preset a1Dg5eFl
```

--------------------------------

### Define Block Registry Entry in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/(root)/_blocks.mdx

Create a block definition object in the registry-blocks.tsx file following the shadcn-ui registry schema. This configuration specifies block metadata, dependencies, file structure, and categories for proper registration and installation.

```typescript
export const blocks = [
  // ...
  {
    name: "dashboard-01",
    author: "shadcn (https://ui.shadcn.com)",
    title: "Dashboard",
    description: "A simple dashboard with a hello world component.",
    type: "registry:block",
    registryDependencies: ["input", "button", "card"],
    dependencies: ["zod"],
    files: [
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx",
        type: "registry:page",
        target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx",
      },
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/hello-world.tsx",
        type: "registry:component",
      },
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/example-card.tsx",
        type: "registry:component",
      },
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/hooks/use-hello-world.ts",
        type: "registry:hook",
      },
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/lib/format-date.ts",
        type: "registry:lib",
      },
    ],
    categories: ["dashboard"],
  },
]
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Various Authentication Error Scenarios in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/registry/authentication.mdx

This TypeScript code provides examples of handling different authentication failure scenarios, such as missing tokens, expired tokens, and insufficient permissions. It returns specific custom error messages to guide users on how to resolve the issue, improving the user experience for authentication problems.

```typescript
// Different error messages for different scenarios
if (!token) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    {
      error: "Unauthorized",
      message:
        "Authentication required. Set REGISTRY_TOKEN in your .env.local file",
    },
    { status: 401 }
  )
}

if (isExpiredToken(token)) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    {
      error: "Unauthorized",
      message: "Token expired. Request a new token at company.com/tokens",
    },
    { status: 401 }
  )
}

if (!hasTeamAccess(token, component)) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    {
      error: "Forbidden",
      message: `Component '${component}' is restricted to the Design team`,
    },
    { status: 403 }
  )
}
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize new shadcn/ui project with preset

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/SKILL.md

Create a new shadcn/ui project with a specified preset. Use `--preset` with a preset code (e.g., `base-nova`) or a custom preset code, and optionally specify a template like `vite`.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset base-nova
```

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --name my-app --preset a2r6bw --template vite
```

--------------------------------

### Displaying Empty States with shadcn/ui Empty Component

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/skills/shadcn/rules/composition.md

This example illustrates the use of the `Empty` component to provide feedback when there is no content to display. It supports `EmptyHeader`, `EmptyMedia`, `EmptyTitle`, `EmptyDescription`, and `EmptyContent` for a rich and actionable empty state. This improves user experience by guiding users on how to populate the empty section.

```tsx
<Empty>
  <EmptyHeader>
    <EmptyMedia variant="icon"><FolderIcon /></EmptyMedia>
    <EmptyTitle>No projects yet</EmptyTitle>
    <EmptyDescription>Get started by creating a new project.</EmptyDescription>
  </EmptyHeader>
  <EmptyContent>
    <Button>Create Project</Button>
  </EmptyContent>
</Empty>
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Laravel App

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/laravel.mdx

Use this command to start a new Laravel project. Ensure you select the React starter kit when prompted.

```bash
laravel new my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize shadcn/ui with Preset

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/installation/laravel.mdx

Run this command to initialize shadcn/ui using a preset generated by shadcn/create. This command assumes you have a preset configured and specified via the --preset flag.

```bash
npx shadcn@latest init --preset [CODE] --template laravel
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vaul Dependency Manually

Source: https://github.com/shadcn-ui/ui/blob/main/apps/v4/content/docs/components/base/drawer.mdx

Install the underlying Vaul library, which the shadcn/ui Drawer component is built upon, as a prerequisite for manual installation.

```bash
npm install vaul
```
